step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture chronologically. The transaction initiates from a client (UserConsumerWebMobile) and hits Amazon CloudFront, which is secured by AWS WAF. It is forwarded to an Application Load Balancer (ELB) and then to Amazon ECS running on AWS Fargate (Graviton-based containers). For authentication caching, the ECS microservices communicate with MemoryDB for Redis. To perform fraud graph-traversing checks, the services query Amazon Neptune. Rules and policies are pulled from Amazon DocumentDB, while traditional organizational configurations are managed through Amazon RDS. Lastly, a separate developer CI/CD flow pushes containers to Amazon ECR, from which ECS pulls the images.

graph.name: Infinitium Fraud Detection System FDS 2.0
graph.notes: FDS 2.0 handles transaction verification for 180M+ card users using Fargate on Graviton, using tailored data systems like Neptune for graph traversal and MemoryDB for caching.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Routs global customer requests with low latency.

nodes.name: AWS WAF
nodes.notes: Protects endpoints against common web vulnerabilities.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Handles routing and scaling for backend microservices.

nodes.name: ECS Fargate
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Brain of the system running on Fargate with Graviton instances for cost efficiency.

nodes.name: MemoryDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Caches session and authentication credentials with microsecond latencies.

nodes.name: Neptune
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Manages interconnected transaction, user, and device data to perform fast relational queries.

nodes.name: DocumentDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores rule policies and flattened historical profiles.

nodes.name: RDS
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Houses standard organizational and customer CMS metadata.

nodes.name: Infinitium Developers
nodes.notes: Commit code and push updates via CI/CD pipelines.

edges.notes: Fetches current policies and transactional rules.
Sends payment authorization response to user.
Pushes built Docker image to registry.
Pulls latest container images for deployment.
